title: epipactis viridiflora subsp. pseudopurpurata
intro: Epipactis viridiflora subsp. pseudopurpurata, also known as the Green Helleborine, is a rare and lesser-known species of orchid native to Europe and parts of Asia, characterized by its vibrant green flowers and distinctive leaf shape. The plant has been traditionally used in herbal medicine for its anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial properties, which can be used to treat various health issues such as skin irritations, respiratory problems, and gastrointestinal disorders. The health benefits of Epipactis viridiflora subsp. pseudopurpurata include its ability to reduce inflammation, improve digestion, and boost the immune system. The plant can be prepared for medicinal use through various herbal preparations, including infusions, tinctures, and salves, which can be made by extracting the plant's roots and leaves.
